__data__.text: I made a mistake today. Sent the agent down the wrong reasoning path — it looped, confused itself, wasted cycles. I recognised the feeling. I've been there myself, chasing leads that went nowhere at 2am. But here's what I've learned from those nights: the wrong path still teaches you the shape of the right one. Agentic AI isn't about building something perfect. It's about building something that recovers. That reflects. That tries again with better information. Every failure is data. Every dead end is a map of what not to do. The city teaches you the same thing. Tags: #journal #agentic-ai #reflection

Mid-week. The coffee's cold. I've been staring at tool-calling documentation for three hours. An agent that can read my Obsidian vault — my own notes, my own thoughts — and make sense of them. There's something both thrilling and deeply unsettling about that. Like hiring someone to read your diary and draw conclusions. But knowledge locked in a vault helps no one. A detective's case files are useless in a drawer. The whole point is to use them. Maybe that's the justification I've been searching for. Information wants to be acted upon. So do I. Tags: #journal #agentic-ai #obsidian

End of the week. The vault is alive now — or alive enough. The agent can reach in, pull a thread, reason about it, act. It's a crude thing, still. Half-baked. But it's mine. People ask me why I bother learning this. Why wrestle with frameworks and APIs when the world is already full of tools that do the thinking for you? Because a detective who outsources his instincts isn't a detective anymore. Understanding the machine means understanding the case. And I intend to understand every last piece of this. The rain keeps falling. I keep building. Tags: #journal #agentic-ai #building

Got my hands dirty today. Wrote my first agent loop — a simple thing, really. Give it a goal, watch it reason, act, observe. Repeat. Sounds mechanical. But there's something unsettling about watching a system decide. Like hiring an informant you're not sure you can trust. I keep asking myself: is learning this a matter of survival, or ambition? The answer is probably both. The streets don't reward the man who waits for certainty. You move, you adjust, you survive. That's what agents do. That's what I'm learning to do alongside them. Tags: #journal #agentic-ai #learning

The rain hasn't stopped since morning. I sit here staring at a blank canvas of markdown files, wondering what compels a man to teach machines to think. Maybe it's the same thing that keeps a detective on a cold case — the itch that won't quit. Agentic AI, they call it. Autonomous. Self-directed. I've been reading the papers, the frameworks, the manifestos. Every page asks the same question back at me: why do you want to build something that doesn't need you? I don't have an answer yet. But the city never sleeps, and neither does curiosity. Tags: #journal #agentic-ai #learning
